message:
config+spawn: add per-agent-type default prompts (bd-2ywo)

Add [prompts] config section with cc_default, cod_default, gmi_default
(inline strings or file paths). Default prompts are prepended to spawn
prompts for matching agent types. Also adds --robot-default-prompts
endpoint for querying current defaults.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -546,7 +546,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2yge5","title":"Task: Implement SynthesizerTUI full-screen view in internal/tui/synthesizer/","description":"Implement SynthesizerTUI full-screen view with mode visualization and tier badges.

## Component Overview
A Bubble Tea component that displays reasoning modes assigned to each window in the ensemble, including tier chips.

## Visual Design
```
┌─ Ensemble: arch-review ─────────────────────────────┐
│                                                      │
│  Reasoning Modes                                     │
│  ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━                                     │
│                                                      │
│  [0] F1 Argument Mapping  [CORE]  claude  ████████░░ │
│  [1] E2 Root Cause        [CORE]  codex   ██████░░░░ │
│  [2] F43 Systems Thinking [ADV]   gemini  ██████████ │
│  [3] L3 Meta-cognitive    [CORE]  claude  ░░░░░░░░░░ │
│  [4] H2 Strategic         [CORE]  codex   ░░░░░░░░░░ │
│                                                      │
│  Progress: 2/5 complete                              │
│  Strategy: adversarial                               │
│                                                      │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation
```go
// internal/tui/ensemble_modes.go
type ModeVisualization struct {
    Session     string
    Modes       []ModeState
    Strategy    string
    Width       int
    Height      int
}
```

## Styling
- Use lipgloss for consistent styling with existing dashboard
- Tier chip (CORE/ADV/EXP) subtle color coding
- Mode IDs in bold, descriptions in normal weight
- Agent type in subtle color

## Integration
- Add as new panel type in dashboard layout
- Support focus/unfocus states
- Keyboard navigation between modes
- Click to zoom to window

### File Location
internal/tui/ensemble_modes.go

### Dependencies
- Requires Bubble Tea framework
- Uses lipgloss for styling
- Integrates with dashboard panel system (bd-121pa)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:40:28.902920249Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T19:03:46.789674604Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T19:03:46.789605455Z","close_reason":"Added SynthesizerTUI full-screen Reasoning Modes overlay in dashboard (key 'e'), with tier chips, progress bars, refresh, and zoom","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2yge5","depends_on_id":"bd-121pa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2yi1i","title":"Task: Support bundle privacy-mode enforcement","description":"## Scope
Ensure `ntm support-bundle` respects Privacy Mode semantics when bundling session data.

## Why
Support bundles are intended for safe sharing. Privacy Mode is intended to avoid persistence. The combination must be explicit and unsurprising.

## Required Behavior
- If the target session is privacy-enabled:
  - default behavior must **exclude** any content that could contain user text (pane excerpts, prompt history slices, raw event bodies).
  - allow inclusion only with an explicit, scary override (e.g., `--allow-persist` or a dedicated `--allow-private-content`).
- If privacy mode is enabled globally by default (per spec), the same rules apply.
- The bundle should still be useful in privacy mode:
  - doctor/version/config metadata is OK
  - include redaction summaries if redaction is enabled

## Integration Points
- Reuse the centralized privacy gating decision (bd-26364) rather than re-implementing ad-hoc checks.
- Ensure robot output (if used) reports:
  - whether privacy mode was detected
  - whether private content was included or suppressed
  - any warnings emitted

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bundle generation in privacy mode is safe-by-default and does not silently persist user text.
- CLI prints clear messaging when content was suppressed and how to override.
- E2E coverage:
  - ensure the privacy-mode scenario in bd-fgnlw passes (suppressed by default; allowed only with explicit override).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T04:14:30.215185013Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T02:46:10.360511682Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T02:46:10.360370990Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2yi1i","depends_on_id":"bd-1a542","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2yi1i","depends_on_id":"bd-26364","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2yi1i","depends_on_id":"bd-5h1bi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2yuhk","title":"SUB-EPIC: Unit Tests for Output & Formatting (output, tui)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:00:33.007713119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T08:00:33.007713119Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-2ywo","title":"Add default prompts per agent type - --cc-default-prompt, --cod-default-prompt, --gmi-default-prompt in config","description":"Add default prompts per agent type - configure cc-default-prompt, cod-default-prompt, gmi-default-prompt in config.

## Problem Being Solved

Different agent types benefit from different default prompts. Currently no way to set per-type defaults.

## Config Schema

```toml
[prompts]
cc_default = "You are a Claude Code agent. Focus on code quality."
cod_default = "You are a Codex agent. Be concise and direct."
gmi_default = "You are a Gemini agent. Consider multiple approaches."

# Or use files
cc_default_file = "~/.config/ntm/prompts/cc_default.md"
cod_default_file = "~/.config/ntm/prompts/cod_default.md"
gmi_default_file = "~/.config/ntm/prompts/gmi_default.md"
```

## Behavior
1. On spawn, load default prompt for each agent type
2. Prepend to any user-provided prompt
3. Skip if agent type has no default configured

## CLI Override
```bash
ntm spawn my_session --cc=2 --cc-prompt "Override default"
```

## Robot Mode API
ntm --robot-default-prompts

Returns JSON:
```json
{
  "cc_default": "You are a Claude...",
  "cod_default": "...",
  "gmi_default": "..."
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Config supports cc_default, cod_default, gmi_default strings
2. Config supports cc_default_file, etc. for file-based prompts
3. Default prompts prepended to spawn prompts
4. CLI flags override config defaults
5. Empty default = no prepend
6. Robot mode returns current defaults

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/config/prompts_test.go
- Test loading from string vs file
- Test override priority

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
# [E2E-DEFAULT-PROMPTS] Default Prompts Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-DEFAULT-PROMPTS]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing default prompts"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Config supports --cc-default-prompt in [agents.defaults]
2. Config supports --cod-default-prompt in [agents.defaults]
3. Config supports --gmi-default-prompt in [agents.defaults]
4. Default prompts applied on spawn
5. Command line overrides config
6. Unit tests for config parsing
7. E2E test verifying defaults applied","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:36:04.535347843Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:19:28.139176289Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"SilentMarsh","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:36:04.535347843Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T09:32:39.473657630Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T09:32:39.473574826Z","close_reason":"Implemented per-agent-type default prompts. (1) Added PromptsConfig struct to config with cc_default, cod_default, gmi_default (inline + file) fields. (2) Added ResolveForType method (inline > file > empty). (3) In spawn prompt delivery, default prompt is prepended to pane prompt when configured. (4) Added --robot-default-prompts robot endpoint returning JSON. (5) 11 unit tests: ResolveForType (6 agent types), ResolveFromFile, InlineOverridesFile, MissingFile, AllEmpty. All tests pass, build clean.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2yws9","title":"Task: Unit tests for prompt preflight evaluator","description":"## Scope
Add unit tests for the prompt preflight evaluator.

## Cases
- Oversize warning threshold.
- Secret detection (synthetic fixtures) yields expected finding.
- Destructive command detection yields high-severity finding.
- Preview hash stable for identical input.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Table-driven tests.
- No tmux required.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:26:12.599401101Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-02T03:20:00.250713730Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T03:20:00.250651063Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive table-driven unit tests for preflight evaluator covering: oversize warnings, secret detection with synthetic fixtures, destructive command detection, preview hash stability, result structure validation, helper function integration, and lint package integration.  {"id":"bd-2zkfw","title":"rust_proxy Integration: Transparent Proxy with Automatic Failover","description":"Integrate rust_proxy for transparent API proxying with automatic failover.

## Features

- Transparent proxy for API calls
- Automatic failover between regions
- Rate limit distribution
- Traffic logging

## Robot Mode API

```
ntm --robot-proxy-status
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Proxy daemon detection works
2. Status reporting accurate
3. Failover events logged
4. Dashboard widget shows status
5. Robot mode outputs status JSON
6. Config section for all options
7. Unit tests for all components (80% coverage)
8. E2E tests verify proxy integration","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:44:11.379508054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:06:14.544707016Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zkfw","depends_on_id":"bd-2jsvq","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:05Z","created_by":"import"}]}
